BusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: THE SMITH CENTER FOR THE PERFORMING ARTS

IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: TO INSPIRE ARTISTS, AUDIENCES, AND OUR DIVERSE COMMUNITY THROUGH GREAT PERFORMANCES, ENRICHING EXPERIENCES, AND ENGAGING EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMMING.
IRS990.MissionDesc: TO INSPIRE ARTISTS, AUDIENCES, AND OUR DIVERSE COMMUNITY THROUGH GREAT PERFORMANCES, ENRICHING EXPERIENCES, AND ENGAGING EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMMING.
IRS990.Desc: DESIGNED TO EDUCATE, ENRICH AND ENLIGHTEN THE LIVES OF THE LAS VEGAS VALLEY COMMUNITY BY PRESENTING WORLD CLASS PERFORMING ARTS. THE SMITH CENTER OFFERS A BLEND OF PERFORMANCES BY LOCAL ARTS GROUPS AS WELL AS FIRST RUN TOURING ATTRACTIONS. THE FEATURED MUSIC, THEATER AND DANCE COMPANIES CELEBRATE CULTURES FROM ALL OVER THE WORLD ALONG WITH LOCAL RESIDENT COMPANIES THE LAS VEGAS PHILHARMONIC AND NEVADA BALLET THEATER. THE SMITH CENTER BEGAN OPERATIONS IN MARCH 2012 AND HAS ENRICHED THE LIVES OF AN ESTIMATED 1.8 MILLION PATRONS.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: IN COLLABORATION WITH THE KENNEDY CENTER PARTNERS IN EDUCATION PROGRAM AND WOLF TRAP INSTITUTE FOR EARLY LEARNING THROUGH THE ARTS, WORKSHOPS, ARTIST RESIDENCIES, AND DEMONSTRATION TEACHING SESSIONS WERE CONDUCTED THROUGHOUT THE YEAR TO EDUCATE THE LAS VEGAS VALLEY COMMUNITIES YOUTH THROUGH THE PERFORMING ARTS. NATIONAL AND INTERNATIONAL TOURING THEATER PRODUCTIONS BASED ON CHILDREN'S LITERATURE WERE PRESENTED FOR YOUNG AUDIENCES. PROGRAMMING FOCUSED ON PROVIDING A VARIETY OF PERFORMING ARTS EXPERIENCES FOR NEARLY 650,000 STUDENTS AND EDUCATORS IN PRE-K THROUGH GRADE 12 WITHIN CLARK COUNTY. PROGRAMMING WAS CONDUCTED ONSITE AND IN VENUES THROUGHOUT THE COMMUNITY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: LOBBYING EXPENSES FOR THE CURRENT FISCAL YEAR THAT WERE ACCRUED IN THE PRIOR YEAR AND PAID THIS YEAR. EXPENDITURES AND TIME SPENT LOBBYING WERE INSUBSTANTIAL COMPARED TO TIME/EXPENDITURES SPENT ON TYPICAL ACTIVITIES THE SMITH CENTER ENGAGES IN. THE SMITH CENTER HAS NO PLANS TO CONTINUE TO LOBBY IN THE FUTURE.
ENDOWMENT FUNDS ARE, ASSUMING CONDITIONS ARE MET, USED FOR OPERATING, MAINTAINING, OR EXPANDING THE SMITH CENTER ALONG WITH PROVIDING EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS.

MANAGEMENT EVALUATED TSC'S TAX POSITIONS AND CONCLUDED THAT TSC HAD TAKEN NO UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS THAT REQUIRE ADJUSTMENT TO THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS TO COMPLY WITH THE PROVISIONS OF THIS GUIDANCE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: PART I, LINE 3 - ACTIVITIES PER REGION
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: FOREIGN INVESTMENTS AS FOLLOWS: ANCHORAGE CAPITAL PARTNERS OFFSHORE LTD (CAYMAN ISLANDS) = $1,748,018 HBK MULTI-STRATEGY OFFSHORE FUND LTD (CAYMAN ISLANDS) = $1,824,847 JANA OFFSHORE PARTNERS LTD (CAYMAN ISLANDS) = $2,101,052 MIURA GLOBAL FUND LTD (VIRGIN ISLANDS) = $824,382 OZ OVERSEAS FUND II LTD (CAYMAN ISLANDS) = $127,691 SEG PARTNERS OFFSHORE LTD (CAYMAN ISLANDS) = $2,317,863 VALUEACT CAPITAL INTERNATIONAL I LP (VIRGIN ISLANDS) = $993,930 DBL BLACK DIAMOND LTD IRT MIRRORED HLDG(CAYMAN ISLANDS) = $1,401,800 DBL BLACK DIAMOND LTD SERIES D (CAYMAN ISLANDS) = $324,580 HOLDBACK - OZ OVERSEAS = $11,539 FOREIGN EXPENDITURES AS FOLLOW: INVESTMENT FEES (UNITED KINGDOM) = $8,368 PROGRAM SUPPLIES (UNITED KINGDOM) = $19,000 PROGRAM SUPPLIES (CANADA) = $20,925

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: FUNDS WERE GIVEN TO RECOGNIZE OUTSTANDING TEACHERS AND SCHOOLS WITHIN THE CLARK COUNTY SCHOOL DISTRICT.

OtherNonCashContriTableGrp.Desc: STITCHED AT THE COSMOPOLITAN LAS VEGAS
LAS VEGAS CONVENTION AND VISITORS AUTHORITY

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE SMITH CENTER FOR THE PERFORMING ARTS BOARD OF DIRECTORS ANNUALLY REVIEWS POLICY DOCUMENTS AND INFORMATION REQUIREMENTS THAT ARE REPORTED IN FORM 990. A COPY OF THE COMPLETE FORM 990 IS PROVIDED TO ALL MEMBERS OF THE ORGANIZATION'S AUDIT COMMITTEE AND THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS PRIOR TO SUBMISSION TO THE IRS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ALL OFFICERS, DIRECTORS AND KEY EMPLOYEES REVIEW THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY AND SIGN A STATEMENT REGARDING THE POLICY ANNUALLY. ALL EMPLOYEES SIGN A STATEMENT ACKNOWLEDGING THEY HAVE READ AND UNDERSTAND THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY WHEN THEY ARE HIRED.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE SMITH CENTER FOR THE PERFORMING ARTS BOARD OF DIRECTORS MEETS IN EXECUTIVE SESSION AT LEAST ANNUALLY TO DETERMINE COMPENSATION FOR KEY EMPLOYEES OF THE ORGANIZATION. MEMBERS OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS ARE VOLUNTEERS WHO RECEIVE NO COMPENSATION FROM THE ORGANIZATION IN THEIR ROLES AS OFFICERS OR DIRECTORS. FOR KEY EMPLOYEES, THE BOARD AND THE COMPENSATION COMMITTEE REVIEW COMPARABILITY DATA INCLUDING NATIONAL INDUSTRY SURVEYS AND REGIONAL SURVEYS. THE BOARD ALSO CONTRACTS WITH AN INDEPENDENT THIRD PARTY TO GATHER VERY SPECIFIC COMPARABILITY DATA, REVIEW PROPOSED CHANGES IN COMPENSATION AND MAKE RECOMMENDATIONS REGARDING KEY EMPLOYEE COMPENSATION. INDEPENDENT THIRD-PARTY ORGANIZATIONS ARE ALSO ENGAGED FROM TIME TO TIME TO REVIEW AND DOCUMENT ANY COMPENSATION ARRANGEMENTS BEYOND BASE SALARY FOR COMPARABILITY AND LEGAL COMPLIANCE, ALL EXECUTIVE SESSIONS THAT INVOLVE COMPENSATION DELIBERATION AND DECISIONS ARE CONTEMPORANEOUSLY DOCUMENTED IN MEETING MINUTES. THE SMITH CENTER OBTAINS COMPARABILITY DATA FOR ALL OFFICERS AND KEY EMPLOYEES TO DETERMINE COMPENSATION. THE SMITH CENTER ALSO HAS AN INDEPENDENT COMPENSATION COMMITTEE TO DETERMINE COMPENSATION. THE PROCESS AND PROCEDURE INCLUDES CONTEMPORANEOUS SUBSTANTIATION OF THE DELIBERATION AND DECISION ON THE COMPENSATION.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE SMITH CENTER MAKES ITS GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND AUDITED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.
